notes: Roman figure described in Historia Augusta as considerate, eloquent, and skilled in verse; of noble bearing and some public life involvement.

docs.title: Life of Aelius
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: Furthermore, he was considerate of his family, well-dressed, elegant in appearance, a man of regal beauty, with a countenance that commanded respect, a speaker of unusual eloquence, deft at writing verse, and, moreover, not altogether a failure in public life.
